Scenario.title: Phishing-Driven Ransomware Attack on Energy Sector Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Energy Simple A sophisticated phishing campaign targeting energy sector employees leads to ransomware deployment on critical SCADA systems, disrupting grid operations and demanding financial compensation.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2013-2551 [CWE-XXX]: Suggested exploit for mobile phishing attacks
stage.description: An adversary sends spear-phished emails containing malicious attachments (e.g., fake maintenance reports) to energy grid operators. Recipients are tricked into opening the attachment, enabling initial network access via exploited human error.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-32896 [CWE-XXX]: Exploited vulnerability in document processing
stage.description: A macro embedded in the phishing attachment executes payloads by exploiting unpatched software vulnerabilities (e.g., CVE-2024-32896), allowing remote code execution on the victim’s system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Ransomware (e.g., Maze variant) encrypts critical SCADA system files, disrupting grid operations and demanding payment in cryptocurrency to restore access, consistent with UNC3890’s modus operandi.

Scenario.summary: A targeted phishing campaign compromised energy sector networks, escalating to ransomware deployment that encrypted SCADA systems. The attack leveraged both social engineering and software vulnerabilities to achieve operational disruption and financial extortion.
